title: Prokeep Glossary of Terms
content: Internal Glossary 8x8 (Eight by Eight) - A VOIP Phone provider that often uses Bandwidth lines. We CAN work with 8x8, but it usually requires porting a line. Bandwidth - Underlying telecom group that Prokeep uses to provision all of our customers landline numbers to be able to be texted. BOPIS - Buy online, pickup in store - Also, BORIS: Buy Online Return In Store BODFS: Buy Online Deliver From Store Buying Group - regional or national group of businesses typically operating under a similar model / selling similar products. These groups leverage the (buying) power of all businesses involved to create beneficial relationships with other entities like service providers (what Prokeep is), manufacturers / other suppliers. title: Security and Compliance FAQs
content: A quick reference of questions and answers to common security compliance-related questions for customer facing teams. Please feel free to share answers directly with prospects and customers. If you need somebody to join you on a call or to speak more to any of these topics please reach out to Joshua Crigger our Technical Account Manager. TLS Encryption: Question: How does Prokeep prioritize the security of external data in transit? Answer: Prokeep prioritizes the security of external data through TLS 1.3 encryption. Employs ciphers like TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256, TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384, and TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256. External data is safeguarded using correctly configured RSA 2048-bit certificates issued by the Amazon Certificate Manager (ACM). External

title: Equipment Dealer Industry Info
content: How Prokeep works with Equipment dealerships The equipment and machines that the construction, mining, and agricultural industries rely on are typically bought or rented from an equipment dealership, under an OEM brand like CAT, Komatsu, Bobcat, Vermeer, and Case. Both truck and equipment dealers have similar department structure: sales, parts, and service departments. To minimize risk and maximize success, sellers should be able to define these for every deal at each stage of the as it progresses: Entry Criteria The requirements for the deal to move into the next stage Actions Things done by a prospect and/or seller. Activities that need to be represented during the deal stage. Exit Criteria An activity within the deal associated with the prospect's intent to move forward (next steps). title: Setting Next Steps
content: Using next steps and advanced agreements to create urgency and shorten deal cycle lengths. Training Deck from Q2 2025 Recording of training session from Q2 2025 Handy Dandy Cheat Sheet from PClub.io Main Elements Plan ahead. You should come into all calls with an idea of what next steps will be. "Send me an email," or "touching base in X weeks" are not good plans. Use an advanced agreement and a strong agenda. This aligns the buyer to the purpose of the call and gets them oriented to the future so they're not just passively along for the ride. You should not have a meeting if you can't devise a strong agenda. Identify key timelines early and mention them at every opportunity. "In order to meet your target date of (date)..." title: Guide to Branch Calling
content: This document is to share best practices with Prokeep Sellers about why we do branch calls, how to do branch calls and the benefits of doing so. Why do we make Branch Calls? How can we pitch the solution of Prokeep if we do not know the problems of the distributor? The information gathered from branch calls equips us with the knowledge (pain) to efficiently and effectively book a good meeting. The people that understand the business you're calling on are the ones that work there, they can guide you to the right person effectively cutting down the time it would take for you to find them on your own. title: Buyer's Journey
content: Universal Truths of Sales The vast majority of people you connect with will not buy your product in the next 6 months. In fact, more than half of any given market dont need or dont want your product. The 3% Buyers are more order fulfillment, than sales. True selling is effectively managing the 7% of people who intend to change and the 30% who arent ready, but might act like it. What is a Buyers Journey? A series of stages completed by a person or business when making an abnormal purchase. Think houses, cars, and software not daily stuff The chain of demos, internal approvals, and corporate sign-offs we have to navigate as sellers to predictably and consistently win deals. title: What makes for good Success Criteria?
content: Defining the terms of Success Criteria, a necessity for the transition of a prospect to a customer. What are Success Criteria? Success Criteria are measurable and clearly defined requirements that a project, product, or service must meet in order to be considered successful. They should encompass the minimum requirements from the prospect/customer needed to achieve relevant ROI metrics. It is the responsibility of the sales team member to help the prospect define these metrics during the sales process and then communicate them clearly to the post-sale teams during the transition into Onboarding. What makes for good Success Criteria? Success Criteria should be specific and measurable, and solve a high-value problem for the customer

title: 10DLC Compliance Requirements
content: Understand 10DLC regulations and what customers are required to do in order to be compliant. 10DLC Compliance Requirements One-Pager The telecommunications industry requires specific compliance measures to send 10DLC traffic. These requirements include a Privacy Policy and Terms & Conditions. What is 10DLC? 10DLC stands for '10-Digit Long Code'essentially a 10-digit phone number used for Application-to-Person (A2P) messaging. A2P messaging is typically used by businesses to send messages to customers, whether for marketing, customer service, or other transactional purposes. title: Common Carrier Error Codes
content: This article helps to understand common reasons for message failures. Common BTM Error Codes Service Provider Rejections Error Code Description What It Means 4406 Unallocated To Number The number looks valid, but isnt actually active (yet). 4720 Invalid Destination Address The number isn't able to receive texts  could be a landline, blocked toll-free, or just not set up for messaging. 4730 No Route to Carrier Theres no available path to send the message  the carrier doesnt know how to deliver it. 4750 Message Rejected The carrier declined the message. title: When to Clear Cache and Cookies
content: This wiki explains when it's appropriate to recommend clearing a customer's cache and cookies, what those terms mean, why it works, and when it doesn't. It also outlines best practices for isolating the issue using an incognito or private window before having the customer clear any browser data. What Are Cache and Cookies? Cache: Your browser stores static files (images, stylesheets, scripts) so websites load faster. Over time, these files can become outdated and cause display or loading issues. Cookies: Cookies are small files that store information like login sessions, site preferences, and authentication data. When cookies get corrupted or expired, login issues and inconsistent behavior can occur. Recommended video

title: Support for HEIC Image Files in Prokeep
content: HEIC image files are a type of image file that is used by Apple on their iOS products. They are more efficient than typical jpg or gif images, but they also have inconsistent compatibility across other devices. By default, Windows does not support HEIC. However, there are programs available for Windows (such as GIMP) which support the format. If a Prokeep user is using a compatible OS, they are able to copy the image and paste it in-line into Prokeep using their default image viewer. If a Prokeep user is using Windows, they are able to copy the image and paste it in-line into Prokeep provided that they open the image in a compatible program and copy it from that program. title: Tasks Overview
content: Brief look into the upcoming Tasks capability Prokeep Tasks allows users to create reminders for themselves and others to reach out to contacts. Tasks can be Open, Unassigned, and Closed. Open tasks are tasks that are assigned to a specific person, but not yet completed. Unassigned tasks are assigned to a group, but not a specific person. And closed tasks are tasks that are already completed. Creating a Task To create a task, go to the task page and click Create New Task. Users can also click on the compose button and click the Task Icon. If you assign the task to someone directly, that user will be sent an internal message from Prokeep telling them they have a task. title: Fax
content: Centralized Communication FAQs for CSM and Sales Support Can I add multiple shared email addresses or fax numbers to the same Group/Location? No. Each Group/Location only allows for 1 text number, 1 shared email account (i.e, orders@abcsupply.com or sales@abcsupply.com) and 1 fax line. Members of that Group/Location will be able to view, respond and manage all types of messages in their Group. When would I need to set up a new Group for a communication channel running through Prokeep? If you have more than one text number, shared email account or fax line you want to manage in Prokeep you will need to set up a new Group. title: Module 1: Introduction to Feedback
content: REMINDER: You can choose the learning style that suits you best by either reading the content below OR watching it presented HERE What is Feedback?: We want to start with the definition of feedback, because while it may seem very black and white, everyone has their own definition here. Broadly speaking, Feedback is a response or a signal that causes you to gain insight or otherwise take a different approach to what you are doing. It is information provided to a person about their performance, behavior or actions. Feedback can come in various forms, including evaluative (how you did and where you are), appreciative a.k.a praise,and coaching (how you can improve). No matter what kind of feedback is being given, it is always centered aroun

There is no shortage of feedback frameworks out there to use, a lot of them great, and some that you should never use, more on that later. No matter how it lands in your lap, knowing how to listen to it is just as important as knowing how to give it. Thats where active listening comes into play. Active listening is all about truly hearing what someone is saying. Not just nodding along while your brain is busy planning your next genius responsethis isnt a debate. How to Reward Feedback When you get feedback from another person, particularly criticism, consider yourself lucky and reward it! Rewarding feedback is a vital step in keeping the feedback train going. The person giving the feedback took a big interpersonal risk to tell you the feedback, and if you do not reward that risk in a substantial way, you likely won't get that gift again. So what does rewarding feedback look like? Well it depends on how you interpret the feedback. You are either going to agree and take action, or respectfully disagree. title: Prokeep History
content: Founded in 2016, Prokeep was born out of an excitement to learn firsthand, a desire to create, and a genuine admiration for the people and relationships that are the lifeblood of the construction industry. As new homeowners, our founders noticed their most resourceful contractors would text the personal cell phone of their distributors rep for part availability or technical help. While the intention was to get the job done more efficiently and cost effectively, sometimes the distributor rep wasn't available because he was in the warehouse, out to lunch, or worse-out sick! title: Our Values
content: Our core values are the yardstick by which we measure ourselves. They serve as our mission control for decision making and are ingrained in all we do. Our core values are: Build Together. Own It. Be Humble. Be Human. Have Fun! Build Together Collaboration, camaraderie, and customer-focus are core to how we collectively work and win. What it is What it isn't . We are in it together and welcome opportunities to collaborate with each other. title: Mission
content: We Empower Distributors to Win. Everything starts with our mission, to empower our customers by giving them the tools they need to stay competitive. But what does this really mean? In a world where there is a constant push to replace relationships and human interactions with technology, this approach applies to distribution as much as anywhere else. Significant effort is being put into building marketplaces that bypass distributors or create fully automated ordering systems to make transactions as quick as possible. While this may be efficient, it often results in a poor customer experiencelacking tailored solutions, the extra effort to make things right, and the human touch. However, this doesn't negate the real threat or the power of technology. That's

title: Vision
content: Prokeep will power the global wholesale economy and allow all distributors to thrive and have a meaningful impact on the people in their communities. Our aim is to transform Prokeep from a simple communication platform into the indispensable tool distributors rely on to streamline interactions and influence purchasing decisions. Key characteristics of a Pal include: Communicator: A Pal should encourage open communication.
